### **Project Summary: A Modern HOA Website**

This project will develop a modern, user-centric website for the HOA, transforming it from a static information hub into an interactive platform that facilitates communication, volunteerism, and resident services. A modern website is defined by its ability to engage users through two-way communication, its seamless functionality across all devices, and its clean, intuitive design. This site will not only provide information but also serve as a key tool for the community. The project incorporates feedback from the Tech Committee, ensuring it directly addresses the needs of residents and leadership.

### **Project Outline**

#### **1\. Foundational Elements & Design**

* **Header & Footer:** The header will include the HOA logo and a clear, user-friendly navigation menu. The **VIS Portal** link should be clearly labeled and available from every page. The footer will contain essential legal information, copyright, and contact details.  
* **Navigation:** The navigation is redesigned based on user needs and modern UI best practices.  
  * **Full Navigation Outline:**  
    * **Home** (link to homepage)  
    * **About** (link to about page)  
      * Board Members  
      * Board Candidates  
      * HOA Mission  
      * Flora, Fauna, and Yard Care  
      * Security?  
    * **Committees** (dropdown menu)  
      * Architectural (link to page)  
      * Care  
      * Code Enforcement (link to page)  
      * Compliance (link to page)  
      * Events  
      * Finance  
      * Gardens  
      * Geese  
      * Governance  
      * Technology  
      * Urban Forest  
    * **Amenities** (link to amenities page)  
      * Amenities Reservation Form  
      * Main Rec  
      * Sports Park  
      * Westside Park  
      * Tot Lot  
      * Security?  
    * **Events** (link to events calendar)  
    * **Government** (dropdown menu)  
      * Board Meeting: Link and Issues  
      * Meeting Minutes (direct link)  
      * Bylaws  
      * Covenants  
      * Policy  
      * Financials  
      * Contact the Board  
    *  **Actions**  
      * **New Neighbor Forms**  
      * **Update Neighbor Forms**  
      * **Appeal Forms**  
      * **Architectural Review**  
      * **Reserve a Garden Plot**  
      * **Reserve a Park**  
      * **Get Engaged \- Volunteer**  
    * **News** (link to announcements page)  
      * Announcements  
      * Classifieds  
      * Recommendations/Referrals  
      * Full Newsletters  
    * **Contact** (link to contact page, positioned as the right-most item)  
    * **VIS Portal** (link to portal, available on every page)

* **Design & User Experience:** The layout will have a bold design with clear sections. Buttons will be added to sections to guide user actions. White space will be used to improve readability and create a clean layout. Redundancy is considered helpful for the user, so some key information may appear in more than one place.

#### **2\. Core Pages & Content**

* **Homepage:** The central hub, providing a summary of key information.  
  * The Announcements section will feature the next meeting date and a Zoom link, as well as a button to submit an announcement, which leads to a form for review by a board member or Michele.  
  * The Events section will include a top-of-section button to submit a new event for review or to reserve common areas like the park.  
* **Announcements Page:** This page will serve as an archive of all news and updates. There will be a separate page for archived announcements. A form for submitting classifieds will be included, as well as a place to subscribe to the newsletter  
* **Events Page:** A **calendar** should be at the top or just below a featured events section with upcoming events. The board meeting Zoom link will also be included here.  
* **Amenities Page:** Provides descriptions and updates on amenities, with a link to the reservation form.  
* **About/Committees:** This section will explain the HOA's structure to encourage engagement.  
* **Resources Page:** A central hub for all official documents, with a direct link to meeting minutes due to high user demand.  
* **Contact Page:** A good place to add the newsletter sign-up.  
* **New Neighbor User Flow:** This user path will include a setup checklist and a brief form that is sent to Erin and a volunteer coordinator. The goal is to connect new neighbors with the community right away.

#### **3\. Forms**

Forms will be used liberally throughout the website to connect users with relevant parties with direct questions. For example, a default contact form will have the following fields:

Name  
Phone   
Email  
Message (with default like *“I want to support the xxx committee”*)  
Submit Button

The backend should check for valid dates/times/number guests, then present instructions while sending email to the secretary and additional relevant parties. Instructions would include response time and any additional instructions relevant to park reservations. This is also a place where we could collect reservation fees, if applicable?

Committees would be invited to write copy and choose form fields for their specific needs.

#### **4\. Login**

# **Home**

I needed to play with Figma to really start mapping out content. I used Caleb’s Wordpress layout without being exceedingly particular, and came out with this:  
![][image1]  
\-\> I added the ‘Event Board’ to the top because I hoped it was a nice callback to the sign at the front of the neighborhood. They light up when you hover\!. I think events at the top is important.  
\-\> I overlapped the images into the section above to draw the eye across the page.

# **About**

#### **Anchors: About, The LCC Board, VIS**

Tucked away in the heart of West Olympia, the Lakemoor Community at Ken Lake is an urban forest community organized around a private natural lake.  
   
In addition to protecting the lake and providing access through neighborhood parks, we come together several times each year for various events, such as the annual Fishing Derby and the 4th of July parade and picnic.  
   
The Lakemoor Community at Ken Lake is a diverse, secure neighborhood which cherishes engagement and connection. 

#### 

## **The LCC Board**

### **Purpose**

The LCC board manages our community resources to care for our lake, our shared public parks, and to fund community-led events. 

### **Structure**

Our HOA is managed by a volunteer board of directors who do the essential work of organizing our community. There are 7 members on the board, with 3 or 4 members elected into office each year. Learn more about board structure by reviewing our bylaws.

The work of the board may be carried out by board members, or by committees. Committees are formed by neighbors around goals or tasks, and are approved by the board. You can join a committee to share your experience, or to volunteer in small ways \- we all work together to care for Ken Lake.

#### **Committees Overview**

| Architectural Compliance Committee (ACC) | Approves or denies exterior construction in keeping with neighborhood covenants. |
| :---- | :---- |
| Care | Supports neighbors who need a little love. |
| Code Enforcement |  |
| Compliance | Reviews compliance issues to determine if enforcement is necessary. |
| Events | Sets event calendar and supports event organizers. |
| Finance | Supports the treasurer to build and monitor the annual budget. |
| Gardens | Maintains and distributes the community gardens in tot lot. |
| Geese | Patrols for geese to deter noise and damage to the docks. |
| Governance  | Reviews bylaws, covenants, and policy to ensure best practice and make recommendations. |
| Technology | Recommends and designs communication systems to keep neighbors connected and organize the wealth of information we share. |
| Urban Forest | Maintains the Urban Forest trails. |

### **Ken Lake Action Team**

The LCC contracts essential work. Meet Michele, Randy, and Mike\!

….

### **VIS**

VIS is management support for HOAs like ours. Access the VIS Portal Here.

# **Information Pages (layout?)**

![][image2]  
\-\> We have \*\*lots\*\* of pdfs, and it would be impractical for us to copy all of the information into a static page  
\-\> Added a to-do list image or component; however not every page needs a component like this  
\-\> Here, overlapping images might be intrusive? I like it some to break standard form, but not as much as on the home page

# **Board Members**

Ken Lake’s LCC is organized by a dedicated volunteer team of board directors. Board directors serve a two year term, and select positions based on time and skill of the current set of directors. Elections are held in November each year. You can learn more about board organization in our neighborhood bylaws.

## **Board Member**

Biography about the board member. Should note relevant experience in leadership and volunteer work, as well as interest, and why working on the board is important to this member.

These bios can either stack or mirror direction down the page (left or right image).

**Contact Information**  
Email \- [boardmember@kenlake.org](mailto:boardmember@kenlake.org)

## 

# **Mission and Vision**

This needs to be written by the board. It should include the mission, or goals, of the organization, as well as a descriptive and strategic vision about how we are achieving those goals.

For example, many respondents to our poll said that the sole mission of LCC was to protect and preserve the lake.

A vision might be “Protecting and preserving the lake through strong community ties. Engagement is harnessed early and often through central communication, events, public spaces, and volunteer committees.”

We can produce a graphic demonstrating how the vision supports the mission.

# **Flora, Fauna, and Yard Care**

As above

# **Security**

Ken Lake Security has served the residents of Ken Lake since May 2005\. It is my goal to keep Ken Lake a great place to live and play. It is my duty to see that the residents of Ken Lake are as secure and safe as possible. It is my responsibility to enforce the rules and regulations set by the Lakemoor Community Board." \- Ralph Oliver, Ken Lake Security  
   
As part of the special focus on enforcing community rules, compliance checks are conducted in recreation areas during the high use periods. Ken Lake Security also monitors vehicle speeds year round to help keep our residents safe.  
   
Ken Lake Security is not a 24-hour service, although security patrols are day and night. To provide a high level of security when Ken Lake Security is not available, a close association has been developed between Ken Lake Security and the Olympia Police Department.”

## **Connected Communities are Safe Communities**

Lakemoor Community Club offers events throughout the year to connect neighbors with neighbors \- because communities where we know each other are just safer. Additionally, neighbors can support each other through our Care Committee \- we are each other’s village here.

## **Personal Safety**

### **Neighborhood Safety**

* Drivers should stay alert and **never** drive faster than 25mph through the neighborhood.  
* If you can, park in a driveway to make more space for pedestrians in the street.  
* If you are parked on the street, check for bikers before opening your car door.  
* Bike with the flow of traffic, and always wear a helmet.  
* Walk opposite traffic so you can see cars coming towards you.  
* Keep dogs leashed for their safety and the safety of others.

### **Lake Safety**

* Always let someone know when you are in the water.  
* Never let children swim unsupervised.  
* Wear a lifejacket when boating, or anytime you may tire.  
* If swimming laps, have a floating device with you.  
* Do not fish near swimming areas where children play. This is their space.

### **Trail Safety**

* Hike or jog with a partner

### **Home Safety**

* Have a fire extinguisher on each level of the home, easily accessible.  
* Have escape ladders available in each upper floor bedroom.

### **In an Emergency**

* Use the panic button on your car to get attention.  
* Ask for help specifically, and by name if you can.

* **Committees** (dropdown menu) (clicking ‘Committees’ links to ‘About’ page)
